steps.title: Use Desmos regression to avoid FOIL mistakes
steps.content:
For a multiple-choice equivalent-expression question, test the expression at a few x-values and fit the resulting quadratic. Enter these in Desmos:
$$x_1=[-1,0,1]$$
$$y_1=(2x_1-3)(x_1+8)-3(6x_1-9)$$

steps.title: Fit the quadratic form
steps.content:
Now regress those points against $ax^2+bx+c$:
$$y_1\sim ax_1^2+bx_1+c$$
Desmos returns $a=2$, $b=-5$, and $c=3$.

steps.title: Match the result
steps.content:
So:
$$f(x)-g(x)=2x^2-5x+3$$
That matches choice B, so B is correct. Expanding manually gives the same result: $(2x-3)(x+8)=2x^2+13x-24$ and $3(6x-9)=18x-27$.
